rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks within the appropriate component scope.
Use assume/guarantee declarations for interface behavior in component types, and assign for implementation-level bindings in component implementations.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and component ports in AGREE expressions only when they are explicitly declared in the model.
Ensure that assumptions and guarantees are scoped to the component type when describing interface behavior.
Constants and equations in AGREE should reference named values or visible features from the model.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or resource constraints directly in AGREE unless modeled as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes assume "label": expression; guarantee "label": expression; eq name : type = expression; const name : type = value; assign output = expression;.
Component type annexes are for interface-level assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not reference AADL properties such as Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE variables
Model fusion and architecture redesign patterns
